carc.description: Non-covered service — payer determined service not medically necessary.
carc.appealNotes: Frequently overturned when admitting diagnosis clinically supports the service. Cite ACEP or specialty guidelines. ER visits for infectious, cardiac, or neurologic diagnoses are almost always upheld. Request peer-to-peer review between your physician and the reviewing physician.

patientFacingSummary: Your insurer denied $2127 that may be wrongful — you have 2 appealable denials with a strong chance of reversal. We've drafted a ready-to-send appeal letter to help you recover this amount.
appealLetter:
**INTERNAL APPEAL — LEVEL 1**

June 28, 2026

Aetna Appeals and Grievances
[Insurer Address]

**RE: Appeal of Denied Claim**
**Claim ID:** 48291
**Member ID:** AET-2204819334
**Date of Service:** June 14, 2026
**Procedure Code:** 99285

Dear Appeals Review Committee:

I am writing to formally appeal the denial of the above-referenced claim for emergency department services rendered at Memorial Hospital on June 14, 2026. The claim was denied under code CO-50, with the stated reason that the service was "not medically necessary."

I respectfully disagree with this determination. I presented to the emergency department with symptoms subsequently diagnosed as pneumonia (ICD-10: J18.9). Pneumonia is a serious respiratory infection that can rapidly progress to respiratory failure, sepsis, and death without prompt evaluation and treatment. The American College of Emergency Physicians recognizes that acute respiratory symptoms consistent with pneumonia warrant emergency evaluation, including diagnostic imaging and laboratory studies, to assess severity and initiate appropriate treatment. A high-complexity emergency visit (99285) was entirely appropriate given the clinical presentation and decision-making required.

**Pursuant to my rights under ERISA §503 and applicable state insurance regulations, I formally request the following information regarding the physician who reviewed and denied this claim:** the reviewer's full name and professional title; medical license number and state(s) of licensure; board certifications and specialty area(s); and confirmation of whether they personally reviewed my complete medical record. As this denial rests on a clinical determination of medical necessity, it must be made by a qualified peer physician in a relevant specialty. A mismatch in specialty constitutes grounds for appeal.

I respectfully request that Aetna reconsider this denial and provide a written determination within 30 days.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Jane Q. Smith
Member ID: AET-2204819334
